title: hieracium chlorophyton subsp. putnokense
intro: Hieracium chlorophyton subsp. putnokense, also known as Putnok hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Putnok hawkweed is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically validated.
